mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which negatively impact corporate earnings and drive S&P 500 forward returns lower over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high UNRATE growth periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and GDP signals strong supply-side momentum, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods of co-expansion in INDPRO and GDPC1 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with high federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signals over-tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations and reduces S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300 thousand units signals weak demand for residential construction, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls < 1300 thousand units in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in low HOUST periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates overheating demand, which prompts monetary tightening and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high RSAFS/CPIAUCSL regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising GDP (GDPC1) combined with slowing industrial production (INDPRO) signals economic overheating, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in late-cycle regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15.
